narrative: Trump-Xi summit headlines and upbeat China trade data dominate the weekend tape, setting up a pro-growth backdrop for the Tokyo open. EUR/USD closed Friday at 1.179; the +175bp Fed-ECB carry advantage likely to cap upside despite recent USD softening. USD/JPY closed Friday at 156.62; easing US-Iran tensions are likely to support the pair at Sunday's open as risk-premium demand recedes. RBNZ hike bias and strong NZD momentum set up NZD/CAD for outperformance at the open, anchored by a 1.631% G8 strength spread. Focus shifts to the RBNZ meeting on May 27 for the next key policy catalyst.
